passage_title: A neuroscientist reveals how to think differently

sentences.sentence: In the last decade a revolution has occurred in the way that scientists think about the brain.
sentences.translation: 过去十年间，科学家思考大脑的方式发生了一场革命。

structure.text: in the way that scientists think about the brain
that scientists think about the brain

difficult_points: 现在完成时表示这场变革发生于过去十年并影响至今。
that 从句修饰 the way，说明科学家思考大脑的具体方式。

sentences.sentence: We now know that the decisions humans make can be traced to the firing patterns of neurons in specific parts of the brain.
sentences.translation: 我们现在知道，人类作出的决定可以追溯到大脑特定区域中神经元的放电模式。

structure.text: that the decisions humans make can be traced to the firing patterns of neurons in specific parts of the brain

difficult_points: humans make 是省略 that/which 的定语从句，修饰 decisions。
can be traced to 是被动结构，表示决定可在神经活动中找到根源。

sentences.sentence: These discoveries have led to the field known as neuroeconomics, which studies the brain's secrets to success in an economic environment that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors.
sentences.translation: 这些发现催生了神经经济学领域；它研究在一个要求创新、要求做事方式不同于竞争者的经济环境中，大脑取得成功的奥秘。

structure.text: These discoveries have led to the field
which studies the brain's secrets to success
that demands innovation and being able to do things differently from competitors

difficult_points: known as ... 后置修饰 field；which 引导非限制性定语从句，先行词仍是 field。
that 从句修饰 economic environment，demands 后并列 innovation 与 being able to ...。
differently from competitors 修饰 do things，强调做事方式有别于竞争者。

expressions.meaning_zh: 以不同于竞争者的方式

sentences.sentence: A brain that can do this is an iconoclastic one.
sentences.translation: 能够做到这一点的大脑，就是具有打破常规特质的大脑。
difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 A brain，this 回指创新并采取不同做法。
one 替代 brain，iconoclastic 表示敢于打破传统观念的。

expressions.meaning_zh: 具有打破常规特质的大脑

sentences.sentence: Briefly, an iconoclast is a person who does something that others say can't be done.
sentences.translation: 简而言之，打破常规者就是去做别人声称不可能做到之事的人。

structure.text: that others say can't be done

difficult_points: who 从句修饰 a person，that 从句再修饰 something，形成嵌套。
others say 后省略引导词 that；can't be done 的主语是关系代词 that。

sentences.sentence: This definition implies that iconoclasts are different from other people, but more precisely, it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways: perception, fear response, and social intelligence.
sentences.translation: 这一定义意味着打破常规者与他人不同；但更准确地说，真正存在三方面差异的是他们的大脑：感知、恐惧反应和社会智能。

structure.text: that iconoclasts are different from other people
it is the brains that are different in three distinct ways
perception, fear response, and social intelligence

difficult_points: but 后使用 it is ... that ... 强调句，强调不同的是 brains。
冒号后的三项具体解释 three distinct ways。

sentences.sentence: Each of these three functions utilizes a different circuit in the brain.
sentences.translation: 这三种功能各自使用大脑中的不同回路。
difficult_points: Each 是主语中心词，因此谓语使用单数 utilizes。
these three functions 回指感知、恐惧反应和社会智能。

expressions.text: a different circuit in the brain

sentences.sentence: Naysayers might suggest that the brain is irrelevant, that thinking in an original, even revolutionary, way is more a matter of personality than brain function.
sentences.translation: 质疑者或许会说，大脑与此无关，并认为以原创乃至革命性的方式思考，更多是个性问题而非大脑功能问题。

structure.text: that thinking in an original, even revolutionary, way is more a matter of personality than brain function
thinking in an original, even revolutionary, way

difficult_points: 两个 that 从句并列作 suggest 的宾语，第二个 that 没有省略。
thinking ... 是动名词短语作第二个从句的主语。
more a matter of A than B 表示“更多是A而不是B的问题”。

sentences.sentence: But the field of neuroeconomics was born out of the realization that the physical workings of the brain place limitations on the way we make decisions.
sentences.translation: 但神经经济学正是源于这样一种认识：大脑的生理运作会限制我们的决策方式。

structure.text: was born out of the realization
that the physical workings of the brain place limitations on the way we make decisions

difficult_points: that 从句是 realization 的同位语，说明这一认识的具体内容。
the way 后的 we make decisions 是省略 in which/that 的定语从句。

sentences.sentence: By understanding these constraints, we begin to understand why some people march to a different drumbeat.
sentences.translation: 通过理解这些限制，我们开始明白为什么有些人会按照不同的节奏行事。

structure.text: why some people march to a different drumbeat

difficult_points: By + 动名词说明获得理解的途径。
why 从句作 understand 的宾语；march to a different drumbeat 是比喻，指不随大流。

expressions.meaning_zh: 按不同方式行事；不随大流

sentences.sentence: The first thing to realize is that the brain suffers from limited resources.
sentences.translation: 首先要认识到的是，大脑受限于有限的资源。

structure.text: that the brain suffers from limited resources

difficult_points: to realize 修饰 thing，说明需要认识的事项。
that 从句作表语，说明 The first thing 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: It has a fixed energy budget, about the same as a 40 watt light bulb, so it has evolved to work as efficiently as possible.
sentences.translation: 大脑的能量预算是固定的，大约相当于一只40瓦灯泡，因此它进化出了尽可能高效的工作方式。

structure.text: It has a fixed energy budget
about the same as a 40 watt light bulb
to work as efficiently as possible

difficult_points: about the same as ... 是插入的补充比较，说明 energy budget 的大小。
so 引出结果；to work ... 说明进化形成的工作方式。

sentences.sentence: This is where most people are impeded from being an iconoclast.
sentences.translation: 这正是大多数人成为打破常规者时受到阻碍的地方。

structure.text: where most people are impeded from being an iconoclast

difficult_points: where 从句作表语，This 回指大脑追求效率这一机制。
be impeded from doing 表示被阻止做某事，being an iconoclast 作介词宾语。

sentences.sentence: For example, when confronted with information streaming from the eyes, the brain will interpret this information in the quickest way possible.
sentences.translation: 例如，当面对从眼睛不断传来的信息时，大脑会用尽可能快的方式解读这些信息。

structure.text: when confronted with information streaming from the eyes
will interpret this information in the quickest way possible

difficult_points: when confronted ... 省略 the brain is，是被动的时间状语结构。
streaming from the eyes 修饰 information；possible 后置修饰 the quickest way。

sentences.sentence: Thus it will draw on both past experience and any other source of information, such as what other people say, to make sense of what it is seeing.
sentences.translation: 因此，大脑会同时利用过去的经验和其他任何信息来源，例如别人的说法，来理解自己正在看到的东西。

structure.text: it will draw on both past experience and any other source of information
such as what other people say
to make sense of what it is seeing

difficult_points: both ... and ... 连接大脑利用的两类信息；such as 后的 what 从句是举例。
两个 what 都相当于 the thing(s) that，分别作 such as 和 of 的宾语。
to make sense of ... 表示大脑利用这些信息的目的。

sentences.sentence: This happens all the time.
sentences.translation: 这种过程无时无刻不在发生。
difficult_points: This 回指大脑借助经验和外部信息快速解释感官输入的过程。
all the time 表示持续、频繁发生，而非某一具体时刻。

sentences.sentence: The brain takes shortcuts that work so well we are hardly ever aware of them.
sentences.translation: 大脑采用的捷径非常有效，以至于我们几乎从未意识到它们的存在。

structure.text: we are hardly ever aware of them

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 shortcuts。
so well 后省略结果连词 that，引出 we are hardly ever aware ...；them 指 shortcuts。

sentences.sentence: We think our perceptions of the world are real, but they are only biological and electrical rumblings.
sentences.translation: 我们以为自己对世界的感知是真实的，但它们其实只是生物和电活动产生的微弱信号。

structure.text: our perceptions of the world are real
but they are only biological and electrical rumblings

difficult_points: think 后省略宾语从句引导词 that。
they 回指 perceptions；rumblings 在此比喻大脑中的生物与电活动。

sentences.sentence: Perception is not simply a product of what your eyes or ears transmit to your brain.
sentences.translation: 感知并不只是眼睛或耳朵向大脑传递信息的产物。

structure.text: is not simply a product of
what your eyes or ears transmit to your brain

difficult_points: not simply 为后一句强调大脑加工预留转折空间。
what 从句作介词 of 的宾语，what 在从句中作 transmit 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: More than the physical reality of photons or sound waves, perception is a product of the brain.
sentences.translation: 与其说感知来自光子或声波的物理现实，不如说它是大脑加工的产物。

structure.text: More than the physical reality of photons or sound waves
is a product of the brain

difficult_points: More than 在此表达侧重点对比，强调大脑比物理刺激本身更能决定感知。
the physical reality of photons or sound waves 指外界客观的视觉和听觉刺激。

sentences.sentence: Perception is central to iconoclasm.
sentences.translation: 感知是打破常规思维的核心。
difficult_points: be central to 表示对某事具有核心作用。
iconoclasm 在本文指挑战惯例、形成不同认识的思维特质。

sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts see things differently from other people.
sentences.translation: 打破常规者看待事物的方式与他人不同。
difficult_points: see things 指理解、看待事物，而非单纯视觉活动。
differently from 与比较对象 other people 构成方式对比。

sentences.sentence: Their brains do not fall into efficiency pitfalls as much as the average person's brain.
sentences.translation: 他们的大脑不像普通人的大脑那样容易陷入追求效率的陷阱。

structure.text: do not fall into efficiency pitfalls
as much as the average person's brain

difficult_points: as much as 比较两类大脑陷入效率陷阱的程度。
the average person's brain 中 person's 为所有格；句末省略 falls into efficiency pitfalls。

sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts, either because they were born that way or through learning, have found ways to work around the perceptual shortcuts that plague most people.
sentences.translation: 打破常规者或是天生如此，或是通过学习，已经找到了绕开那些困扰大多数人的感知捷径的方法。

structure.text: either because they were born that way or through learning
to work around the perceptual shortcuts

difficult_points: either ... or ... 连接两种来源：天生如此或后天学习。
to work around ... 修饰 ways；that 从句修饰 perceptual shortcuts。

sentences.sentence: Perception is not something that is hardwired into the brain.
sentences.translation: 感知并不是一种与生俱来、固定嵌入大脑的东西。

structure.text: that is hardwired into the brain

difficult_points: that 引导定语从句修饰 something。
be hardwired into 比喻某种能力先天固定在大脑中。

sentences.sentence: It is a learned process, which is both a curse and an opportunity for change.
sentences.translation: 它是一个后天习得的过程，这既可能成为束缚，也提供了改变的机会。

structure.text: which is both a curse and an opportunity for change

difficult_points: It 指 perception；learned 表示后天习得的。
which 指整个 learned process，both ... and ... 连接两个表语。

sentences.sentence: The brain faces the fundamental problem of interpreting physical stimuli from the senses.
sentences.translation: 大脑面临一个根本问题：如何解释来自各感官的物理刺激。

structure.text: of interpreting physical stimuli from the senses

difficult_points: of interpreting ... 说明 problem 的具体内容，其中 interpreting 是动名词。
from the senses 修饰 physical stimuli，说明刺激的来源。

sentences.sentence: Everything the brain sees, hears, or touches has multiple interpretations.
sentences.translation: 大脑所看到、听到或触碰到的一切，都可能有多种解释。

structure.text: the brain sees, hears, or touches

difficult_points: the brain sees ... 是省略 that 的定语从句，修饰 Everything。
sees、hears、touches 三个谓语并列，共用主语 the brain 和省略的宾语关系词。

sentences.sentence: The one that is ultimately chosen is simply the brain's best theory.
sentences.translation: 最终被选中的那一种解释，只不过是大脑认为最合理的理论。

structure.text: is simply the brain's best theory

difficult_points: The one 代替 one interpretation；that 从句修饰它。
is ... the brain's best theory 强调感知是大脑的最佳推测，而非客观现实本身。

sentences.sentence: In technical terms, these conjectures have their basis in the statistical likelihood of one interpretation over another and are heavily influenced by past experience and, importantly for potential iconoclasts, what other people say.
sentences.translation: 从专业角度说，这些推测以一种解释胜过另一种解释的统计概率为基础，并且深受过去经验以及——对潜在的打破常规者尤其重要的是——他人说法的影响。

structure.text: have their basis in the statistical likelihood of one interpretation over another
and are heavily influenced by past experience and, importantly for potential iconoclasts, what other people say

difficult_points: have their basis in 与 are influenced by 是共用主语的并列谓语。
one interpretation over another 表示一种解释相较另一种更可能。
what other people say 是融合关系从句，与 past experience 并列作 by 的宾语；中间插入 importantly ...。

sentences.sentence: The best way to see things differently from other people is to bombard the brain with things it has never encountered before.
sentences.translation: 要以不同于他人的方式看待事物，最好的办法就是让大脑大量接触从未遇到过的东西。

structure.text: The best way to see things differently from other people
is to bombard the brain with things

difficult_points: 两个不定式分别修饰 way 和作表语，主干为 The best way is to bombard ...。
it has never encountered before 是省略 that 的定语从句，it 指 the brain。

sentences.sentence: Novelty releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience and forces the brain to make new judgments.
sentences.translation: 新奇事物能让感知过程挣脱过去经验的束缚，并迫使大脑作出新的判断。

structure.text: releases the perceptual process from the chains of past experience
and forces the brain to make new judgments

difficult_points: release A from B 与 force A to do 是两个并列谓语结构。
the chains of past experience 是比喻，指既往经验对感知的束缚。

sentences.sentence: Successful iconoclasts have an extraordinary willingness to be exposed to what is fresh and different.
sentences.translation: 成功的打破常规者有着非同寻常的意愿，愿意接触新鲜而不同的事物。

structure.text: to be exposed to what is fresh and different

difficult_points: to be exposed to ... 修饰 willingness，使用被动形式表示主动接受接触。
what 从句作介词 to 的宾语，指一切新鲜且不同的事物。

sentences.sentence: Observation of iconoclasts shows that they embrace novelty while most people avoid things that are different.
sentences.translation: 对打破常规者的观察表明，他们拥抱新奇事物，而大多数人会避开与众不同的东西。
difficult_points: that 从句作 shows 的宾语；while 在其中引出与大多数人的对比。
第二个 that 引导定语从句修饰 things。

sentences.sentence: The problem with novelty, however, is that it tends to trigger the brain's fear system.
sentences.translation: 然而，新奇事物的问题在于，它往往会触发大脑的恐惧系统。

structure.text: that it tends to trigger the brain's fear system

difficult_points: that 从句作表语，说明 problem 的具体内容。
it 指 novelty；tend to 表示通常有某种倾向。

sentences.sentence: Fear is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast and stops the average person in his tracks.
sentences.translation: 恐惧是像打破常规者那样思考的一大障碍，会让普通人当场止步。

structure.text: is a major impediment to thinking like an iconoclast
and stops the average person in his tracks

difficult_points: to 在 impediment to 中是介词，因此后接动名词 thinking。
stop someone in his tracks 是习语，表示使人突然停止行动。

sentences.sentence: There are many types of fear, but the two that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule.
sentences.translation: 恐惧有很多种，但会抑制打破常规思维、且通常令人在应对时感到困难的两种恐惧，是对不确定性的恐惧和对公众嘲笑的恐惧。

structure.text: There are many types of fear
that inhibit iconoclastic thinking and people generally find difficult to deal with
are fear of uncertainty and fear of public ridicule

difficult_points: the two 指两种恐惧，that 在定语从句中既作 inhibit 的主语，又是 find ... difficult to deal with 中被理解的对象。
people generally find difficult to deal with 是省略关系词的并列定语成分，find + 宾语 + difficult 构成复合宾语。
句末两个 fear of 短语是并列表语。

sentences.sentence: These may seem like trivial phobias.
sentences.translation: 这些恐惧看起来或许只是微不足道的恐惧症。
difficult_points: These 回指对不确定性和公众嘲笑的恐惧。
may seem like 表示表面判断，并为后文说明其普遍性作转折。

sentences.sentence: But fear of public speaking, which everyone must do from time to time, afflicts one-third of the population.
sentences.translation: 但对公开演讲的恐惧困扰着三分之一的人口，而每个人都难免偶尔需要公开讲话。

structure.text: which everyone must do from time to time

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，语义上回指 public speaking，作 do 的宾语。
afflict 表示问题长期困扰某人；one-third of the population 是受影响对象。

sentences.sentence: This makes it too common to be considered a mental disorder.
sentences.translation: 这说明它过于普遍，不能被视为一种精神障碍。

structure.text: to be considered a mental disorder

difficult_points: This 回指三分之一人口都受其困扰这一事实。
too ... to ... 表示“过于……而不能……”；it 指 fear of public speaking。

sentences.sentence: It is simply a common variant of human nature, one which iconoclasts do not let inhibit their reactions.
sentences.translation: 它只是人性中一种常见的变体，而打破常规者不会任由这种恐惧抑制自己的反应。

structure.text: It is simply a common variant of human nature
which iconoclasts do not let inhibit their reactions

difficult_points: one 与 a common variant 同位，which 引导定语从句修饰 one。
let + 宾语 + 动词原形中，which 是 let 的宾语，同时是 inhibit 的逻辑主语。

expressions.text: a common variant of human nature

sentences.sentence: Finally, to be successful iconoclasts, individuals must sell their ideas to other people.
sentences.translation: 最后，要成为成功的打破常规者，人们必须让其他人接受自己的想法。

structure.text: must sell their ideas to other people

difficult_points: 句首不定式表示目的或成为成功者的条件。
sell their ideas 是比喻用法，指说服他人接受自己的观点，而非出售商品。

sentences.sentence: This is where social intelligence comes in.
sentences.translation: 这正是社会智能发挥作用的地方。
difficult_points: where 从句作表语，This 回指说服他人接受想法这一任务。
come in 在此表示某种能力在特定环节开始发挥作用。

sentences.sentence: Social intelligence is the ability to understand and manage people in a business setting.
sentences.translation: 社会智能是指在商业环境中理解并管理他人的能力。
difficult_points: to understand and manage ... 修饰 ability，两个动词共用宾语 people。
in a business setting 限定这种社会智能发挥作用的环境。

expressions.text: the ability to understand and manage people
expressions.meaning_zh: 理解并管理他人的能力

sentences.sentence: In the last decade there has been an explosion of knowledge about the social brain and how the brain works when groups coordinate decision making.
sentences.translation: 过去十年间，关于社会脑以及群体协同决策时大脑如何运作的知识出现了爆炸式增长。

structure.text: there has been an explosion of knowledge
about the social brain and how the brain works

difficult_points: there has been 使用现在完成时，表示知识增长持续至今。
about 后并列 the social brain 与 how 从句；when 从句限定大脑运作的情境。

sentences.sentence: Neuroscience has revealed which brain circuits are responsible for functions like understanding what other people think, empathy, fairness, and social identity.
sentences.translation: 神经科学已经揭示，哪些脑回路负责理解他人想法、共情、公平感和社会身份等功能。

structure.text: which brain circuits are responsible for functions
like understanding what other people think, empathy, fairness, and social identity

difficult_points: which 从句作 revealed 的宾语，使用陈述语序。
like 后列举 functions；what 从句作 understanding 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: These brain regions play key roles in whether people convince others of their ideas.
sentences.translation: 这些脑区在人们能否让他人信服自己的想法方面发挥关键作用。

structure.text: in whether people convince others of their ideas

difficult_points: whether 从句作介词 in 的宾语，表示“能否说服”的不确定结果。
convince others of their ideas 表示使他人相信自己的想法。

sentences.sentence: Perception is important in social cognition too.
sentences.translation: 感知在社会认知中同样重要。
difficult_points: too 表示感知不仅对打破常规重要，对社会认知也重要。
social cognition 指理解他人及社会互动信息的认知过程。

sentences.sentence: The perception of someone's enthusiasm, or reputation, can make or break a deal.
sentences.translation: 对某人热情或声誉的感知，可能决定一笔交易的成败。

structure.text: The perception of someone's enthusiasm, or reputation
can make or break a deal

difficult_points: enthusiasm 与 reputation 由 or 并列，都是 perception of 的对象。
make or break 是固定对举，表示决定成功或失败。

sentences.sentence: Understanding how perception becomes intertwined with social decision making shows why successful iconoclasts are so rare.
sentences.translation: 理解感知如何与社会决策交织在一起，可以说明为什么成功的打破常规者如此罕见。

structure.text: Understanding how perception becomes intertwined with social decision making
how perception becomes intertwined with social decision making
why successful iconoclasts are so rare

difficult_points: Understanding ... 是动名词短语作主语，内部 how 从句作其宾语。
why 从句作 shows 的宾语；become intertwined with 表示逐渐交织。

sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts create new opportunities in every area from artistic expression to technology to business.
sentences.translation: 打破常规者在从艺术表达、科技到商业的各个领域创造新机会。

structure.text: in every area from artistic expression to technology to business

difficult_points: from ... to ... to ... 列举 every area 所覆盖的广泛范围。
create new opportunities 概括打破常规思维带来的实际价值。

sentences.sentence: They supply creativity and innovation not easily accomplished by committees.
sentences.translation: 他们带来的创造力和创新，是委员会式集体决策难以实现的。
difficult_points: They 指 Iconoclasts。
not easily accomplished by committees 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 creativity and innovation。

sentences.sentence: Rules aren't important to them.
sentences.translation: 规则对他们来说并不重要。
difficult_points: them 回指 iconoclasts。
be important to someone 表示某事在某人的价值排序中重要。

sentences.sentence: Iconoclasts face alienation and failure, but can also be a major asset to any organization.
sentences.translation: 打破常规者会面临疏离和失败，但也可能成为任何组织的重要财富。

structure.text: but can also be a major asset to any organization

difficult_points: but 连接风险与价值两个对立面，后半句省略主语 Iconoclasts。
asset 在此指能为组织创造价值的人，而非会计意义上的实体资产。

sentences.sentence: It is crucial for success in any field to understand how the iconoclastic mind works.
sentences.translation: 要在任何领域取得成功，理解打破常规者的思维如何运作都至关重要。

structure.text: It is crucial for success in any field
to understand how the iconoclastic mind works

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正主语是后面的不定式短语。
how 从句作 understand 的宾语，使用陈述语序。
